sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports having a real bad low back pain that started a couple of weeks ago after lifting a box of books. He describes the pain as achy and sharp, radiating down his left leg. He denies numbness, tingling, or loss of sensation or motor function. He reports no bowel or bladder control issues. He states that the pain has been getting worse and that he tried ibuprofen and Tylenol, which provided some relief. He admits to inconsistent exercise habits, being a sedentary job worker and an occasional Olympic weightlifter. He played soccer as a midfielder. He is a fan of Tottenham Hotspur.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Blood pressure is 130/70 mmHg. Respiratory rate is 18 breaths per minute. Heart rate is regular and 68 bpm. Lung sounds are clear bilaterally. Palpation reveals midline tenderness at the L4-L5 disc space with right-sided lumbar paravertebral tenderness. Range of motion is preserved. Straight leg test is positive on the left. Neurological exam reveals symmetrical patellar and Achilles reflexes. No decreased sensation is noted.
medical_concepts: midline tenderness at the L4-L5 disc space
midline tenderness at the L4-L5 disc space
